title: asperugalium occidentale
intro:
Asperugalium occidentale does not exist in the botanical world, however I believe you may be referring to Aspergillus occidentalis. 

Aspergillus occidentalis is a type of fungus belonging to the Aspergillus genus, commonly found in decaying organic matter.

This fungus has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, including its antifungal, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Aspergillus occidentalis may include the stimulation of the immune system and the production of certain compounds that could have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

Aspergillus occidentalis can be prepared using various herbal methods, including fermentation, which can be used to produce extracts that may have therapeutic properties.
